articles.title: Legal Dispute Over Amy Winehouse's Belongings: Court Rules Against Father
articles.summary: Mitch Winehouse, the father of late singer Amy Winehouse, lost a High Court case against two of his daughter's friends, Naomi Parry and Catriona Gourlay. He accused them of profiting from the auction of dozens of Amy's belongings in the United States, claiming they concealed the sales. Mitch argued that legal action was his only way to get answers. However, Deputy High Court Judge Sarah Clarke KC ruled against him, stating that with reasonable diligence, he could have discovered the disputed items earlier. The judge noted Mitch's sensitivity about protecting Amy's memory and ensuring financial benefits for the family, but also criticized him as an unreliable witness who dominated situations and failed to check his claims properly before trial. Amy Winehouse, a Grammy-winning artist who died in 2011, was known for her hits like 'Rehab'. Her friends defended themselves by saying the items were either gifts from Amy or already belonged to them, supported by evidence of Amy's routine generosity in giving away clothing she didn't want to wear repeatedly. The court found this consistent with her character, leading to the ruling in favor of Parry and Gourlay.

articles.title: Art Fund Museum of the Year Finalists Announced
articles.summary: The Art Fund has revealed the five finalists for its Museum of the Year award, which includes a £120,000 prize, believed to be the world's largest museum prize. The nominees are Norwich Castle Museum and Art Gallery, Fitzwilliam Museum in Cambridge, The Box in Plymouth, The National Gallery in London, and V&A East Storehouse in London. Art Fund director Jenny Waldman praised the shortlisted sites for their extraordinary creativity and innovation, highlighting their efforts to open up world-leading collections and connect with communities through ambitious exhibitions and programs. Norwich Castle Museum recently reopened after a five-year redevelopment, now showcasing an authentic display of 12th-century life, while The Box, celebrating its fifth birthday, serves as a museum, gallery, and archive for Plymouth's history. The winner will be announced on June 25 at a ceremony at the Cutty Sark in London, with each of the four other finalists receiving £20,000. The prize is funded by Art Fund members who purchase a National Art Pass and other supporters. Last year's winner was Beamish, The Living Museum of the North. This announcement aims to inspire public engagement with museums.

articles.title: Arthur Fils Claims Barcelona Title in Comeback Victory
articles.summary: Arthur Fils, a 21-year-old French tennis player, secured his first ATP title since October 2024 by winning the Barcelona clay-court tournament. He defeated Russian opponent Andrey Rublev in straight sets, 6-2 7-6 (7-2), in the final. This victory marks a significant comeback for Fils, who missed the latter part of the 2025 season due to a back injury. Since returning to the tour in February, he has demonstrated consistent form, reaching the final in Qatar, quarter-finals at Indian Wells, and semi-finals in Miami. Fils expressed gratitude to his team during the trophy presentation, highlighting the eight-month struggle with injury. Rublev commended Fils' performance, calling it 'ridiculous' and acknowledging him as one of the tour's best players. Additionally, in the men's doubles, British pair Julian Cash and Lloyd Glasspool won their first title of 2026, defeating French-Italian duo Pierre-Hugues Herbert and Andrea Vavassori. Cash emphasized the importance of returning to winning ways after previous finals. Fils is expected to re-enter the top 25 world rankings following this success.

articles.title: Rybakina Secures Victory in Stuttgart Tennis Final
articles.summary: Elena Rybakina, the world number two tennis player from Kazakhstan, defeated Karolina Muchova of the Czech Republic in the final of the indoor clay-court tournament in Stuttgart. The match concluded with Rybakina winning in straight sets, 7-5 6-1, lasting one hour and 18 minutes. This victory marks her second title of 2026, following her Australian Open win in January, and her second Stuttgart title overall, having previously won in 2024. A notable aspect of the Stuttgart tournament is the prize: a Porsche car, which Rybakina was unable to drive after her 2024 win due to lacking a driving license. In her post-match comments, Rybakina expressed gratitude to her team for their support in her continuous improvement. Meanwhile, Muchova's performance, including a recent win at the Qatar Open, positions her just outside the top 10 in world rankings, highlighting her competitive rise in women's tennis.

articles.title: Sidemen Charity Match: Fundraising Success with Memorable Highlights
articles.summary: The Sidemen charity match, held at a sold-out Wembley Stadium, successfully raised £6.2 million for charities Bright Side and M7 Education. The event featured a high-scoring game that ended in a 10-10 draw, with the YouTube Allstars winning in a penalty shootout. This seventh edition mixed team members from the Sidemen across both sides, adding a unique twist. Over two million viewers watched the livestream on YouTube, though this was fewer than last year, yet it set a record for funds raised. Key highlights included Max Fosh's trick of setting a yellow card on fire, which amazed the crowd, and Tinie Tempah's halftime performance of hits like Written In The Stars. Organizers, including Sidemen member Ethan Payne, emphasized the event's popularity stems from its lighthearted, 'not serious' atmosphere, blending sports and entertainment to engage a broad audience while supporting charitable causes.

articles.title: Emma Raducanu's Extended Break from Tennis Due to Health Concerns
articles.summary: Emma Raducanu, the British number one tennis player and former US Open champion, has withdrawn from the Madrid Open, extending her absence from the WTA Tour. She has been dealing with a viral illness since February, which forced her to skip recent tournaments like the Miami Open and Linz Open. Raducanu is focusing on her fitness and requires significant training on clay courts before returning to competition. Clay surfaces pose a challenge for her due to past back issues, including an injury in Madrid in 2022 that led to further problems. In 2024, she showed promise on indoor clay but faced a defeat in Madrid, after which she cited mental and emotional exhaustion. Her clay court history includes operations in 2023 and back troubles in events like Strasbourg and Roland Garros. The next opportunity for Raducanu to play on clay is the Italian Open in Rome in May, with potential tournaments in Strasbourg and Rabat before the French Open. If she misses the entire clay season, her break could last three months, impacting her preparation for the grass court season. The latter half of the year is crucial for Raducanu, offering chances to play on grass and hard courts where she has previously excelled.
